questions.explanation: SageMaker Autopilot automatically explores different algorithms and hyperparameters, generating multiple candidate models. It produces a leaderboard ranking models by performance, along with generated notebooks showing the complete ML pipeline. This enables non-ML-experts to build production-quality models.

questions.question: What does SageMaker Experiments help ML teams manage?
options: Tracking, organizing, and comparing multiple training runs and their parameters/metrics
questions.explanation: SageMaker Experiments organizes ML experiments by tracking training runs (trials), their hyperparameters, metrics, and artifacts. It allows data scientists to compare results across runs, reproduce experiments, and maintain an organized record of model development iterations.

questions.question: Which protocol does SageMaker use to encrypt data in transit between the client and inference endpoints?
questions.explanation: SageMaker endpoints use HTTPS (TLS/SSL) to encrypt all data in transit between clients and the inference endpoint. This ensures that inference requests and responses cannot be intercepted or tampered with during transmission. TLS encryption is mandatory and cannot be disabled.

questions.question: An inference endpoint receives requests with varying payload sizes. Some requests take 5 seconds to process, causing timeouts. What feature allows asynchronous processing of long-running inference requests?
questions.explanation: SageMaker Asynchronous Inference queues incoming requests and processes them asynchronously, supporting payloads up to 1 GB and processing times up to 15 minutes. It sends completion notifications via SNS and can scale to zero when idle, making it ideal for long-running or variable-length inference tasks.

questions.question: An ML team wants to use SageMaker Pipelines with a human-in-the-loop approval step before deploying a new model to production. How is this implemented?
options: Use a Lambda step in the pipeline that sends an email
Register the model in Model Registry with 'PendingManualApproval' status; a manual or automated approval step changes status to 'Approved' before the deploy step executes
Use SageMaker Ground Truth for model approval
Configure an SNS topic that pauses the pipeline
questions.explanation: SageMaker Model Registry supports approval workflow statuses: PendingManualApproval, Approved, Rejected. A pipeline registers a new model version with pending status, then a condition step checks the approval status before proceeding to deployment. Approvers can approve via console, API, or automated quality gates.

questions.question: A SageMaker training job needs to access a private dataset in S3 and write model artifacts back to S3. Which IAM configuration is required?
options: Attach the AdministratorAccess policy to the SageMaker role
Create a SageMaker execution role with s3:GetObject for the training data bucket and s3:PutObject for the model artifacts bucket
Create an S3 bucket policy allowing public access
questions.explanation: The SageMaker execution role should follow the principle of least privilege: s3:GetObject permission for the training data bucket and s3:PutObject permission for the model output bucket. Using AdministratorAccess or root credentials violates security best practices and broadens the blast radius if credentials are compromised.

questions.question: A deployed SageMaker endpoint occasionally returns errors during traffic spikes. CloudWatch shows the InvocationsPerInstance metric exceeding 1,000 during spikes. What is the most effective remediation?
options: Configure application auto-scaling with a target tracking policy on InvocationsPerInstance, setting the target below 1,000
Switch to a smaller instance type to save costs
Enable data capture to record the errors
questions.explanation: Application Auto Scaling with a target tracking policy automatically adjusts the number of instances to maintain a target InvocationsPerInstance metric. Setting the target below the failure threshold (e.g., 750) ensures SageMaker adds instances before the endpoint becomes overloaded during traffic spikes.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using SageMaker Inference Recommender before deploying a model to production?
options: It benchmarks the model across different instance types to recommend the best price-performance configuration
It generates API documentation for the endpoint
It validates the model's accuracy on test data
questions.explanation: SageMaker Inference Recommender runs load tests on your model across different instance types and configurations. It provides latency, throughput, and cost metrics for each configuration, helping you select the optimal instance type that meets your latency requirements at the lowest cost.

questions.question: A regulated industry requires that all ML model predictions be explainable — each prediction must include which features contributed most to the decision. Which SageMaker feature provides this in production?
options: SageMaker Clarify integrated with the endpoint for real-time SHAP-based feature attributions
questions.explanation: SageMaker Clarify can be configured to run alongside inference endpoints, computing SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values for each prediction. SHAP values quantify each feature's contribution to a specific prediction, providing the per-prediction explainability required by regulated industries like healthcare and finance.
